article_title: systemd 261 Reaches Its First Release Candidate With a Built-In OS Installer, a Cloud Metadata Subsystem, and Live-Update Kernel Handover

content_preview.title: systemd 261 Reaches Its First Release Candidate With a Built-In OS Installer, a Cloud Metadata Subsystem, and Live-Update Kernel Handover
content_preview.summary: systemd 261-rc1 adds systemd-sysinstall, an IMDS subsystem, a storagectl tool, and PID1 support for the kernel's Live Update Orchestration and Kexec Handover.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The systemd project has published the first release candidate of its next version, systemd 261, tagged [v261-rc1](https://github.com/systemd/systemd/releases/tag/v261-rc1) and dated 22 May 2026. The candidate folds several new subsystems into the Linux init system and service manager, including a built-in textual operating-system installer, a service for reading cloud instance metadata, a unified storage-management tool, and integration with the Linux kernel's live-update and kexec-...

editor_notes.content_quality: Precise, well-structured release-candidate News piece (608 words) on systemd 261-rc1, sectioned by feature and careful to mark the changelog's 'in spe' (forthcoming) status.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-0 (GitHub v261-rc1 release tag) confirms verbatim the systemd-sysinstall quote, 'operates either interactively or command-line driven', the IMDS-subsystem and systemd-imdsd quotes, the storagectl / io.systemd.StorageProvider / systemd-storage-fs@.service quote, and the PID1 LUO/KHO + FD Stores quote; the tag's authoritative datetime attribute is 2026-05-22 (matching 'dated 22 May 2026'). source-1 (systemd NEWS changelog) confirms 'CHANGES WITH 261 in spe', the systemd-sysinstall wrapper quote (systemd-repart / bootctl link / systemd-creds), the full cloud-platform list (Amazon EC2, Microsoft Azure, Google Compute Engine, Hetzner, Oracle Cloud, Scaleway, Tencent Cloud, Alibaba ECS), and the /run/boot-loader-entries/ removal in 262. source-2 (The Register) confirms all four date-of-birth-field quotes verbatim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every component name (systemd-sysinstall, systemd-imdsd, storagectl, io.systemd.StorageProvider, systemd-storage-fs@.service, LUO/KHO) and every quote is verbatim from a primary source (GitHub release tag + NEWS changelog per Rule 9) — no fabricated identifiers in a name-dense article. The DOB field is correctly hedged as contingent-on-not-being-reverted. The internal cross-reference to the systemd 260 article was verified to exist.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; 'What We Don't Know' correctly flags the RC status, the backwards-incompatible changes, and the contingent DOB inclusion.
editor_notes.originality: Legitimate force-follow-up to the April systemd 260 coverage — centers on the new 261-rc1 feature set and cross-references the prior release rather than re-narrating it.
recommendations: Added raw.githubusercontent.com to the allowlist (GitHub's raw-file host, serving the systemd NEWS changelog as a primary source).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-verified release submission with model primary-source discipline. APPROVE.
